title: Creating keys without a rotation period is security-sensitive
description: Keys that are not rotated regularly are more likely to be compromised and abused. Key rotation limits the window of exposure if a key is leaked or stolen. All cryptographic keys should have a defined rotation period to support strong security hygiene.
message: Key does not have a rotation period. Set a rotation schedule for all keys.
impact: Stale or long-lived keys increase the risk of compromise and unauthorized access to sensitive resources.
recommendation: Define a rotation period for all keys (e.g., 90 days). Automate key rotation and monitor for overdue keys.

noncompliant.description: No rotation period is set, so the key may remain active indefinitely.

compliant.description: A 90-day rotation period is set, reducing the risk of key compromise.

devsecops.security: Regular key rotation limits the impact of key compromise and supports incident response.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should enforce key rotation policies and alert on overdue keys.
devsecops.compliance: Key rotation is required by PCI DSS, HIPAA, and other standards.

why_is_this_an_issue: Keys that are not rotated can be abused for long periods if compromised, increasing risk to the organization.
how_to_fix: Set a rotation period for all keys. Automate rotation and monitor for compliance.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
security_mappings.CERT: MSC61-CPP: Do not use deprecated or weak cryptographic algorithms
